matches.x: 1-4 Moroni was exceedingly rejoiced because of the exceeding success which Helaman had had. Moroni received the epistle from Helaman in the early part of the Thirtieth year of the Judges, and upon him reading it he was filled with joy and thanksgiving for he knew that no man without the help of the Lord could accomplish the mighty things that Helaman had brought to an issue of full success. Nor was Moroni alone in his rejoicing, but that all the Nephites in every part of the region round about…

Moroni received an epistle from Helaman. In the beginning of the next year, or the Thirtieth year of the Judges, Moroni received a written account of the war in the southwest from Helaman, the son of Alma. Helaman's greeting to Moroni dispels the idea that there may have been dissension among the Nephites themselves. That Moroni was a spiritual as well as a military leader is evidenced in Helaman's salutation: "My Dearly Beloved Brother, Moroni, as well in the Lord as in the tribulations of our…

22 And now, verily I say unto you, that as I said that I would make known my will unto you, behold I will make it known unto you, not by the way of commandment, for there are many who observe not to keep my commandments.

22. And now, verily, I say unto you, that as I said that I would
 make known my will unto you, behold I will make it known unto you,
 not by the way of commandment, for there are many who observe not
 to keep my commandments.
7 Then.came in the magicians, the astrologers, the Chaldeans, and the soothsayers; and I told the dream before them; but they did not make known. unto me the interpretation thereof.
30 And that they. would not give their daughters unto the people of the land, nor take the daughters of the people for their sons.

5-8 The people of Nephihah were attacked by the Lamanites. Inspired and inspirited by Helaman's success in regaining from the Lamanites the lands and cities of the Nephites situated in the southwestern sector of greater Zarahemla, Moroni, after sending his epistle to Pahoran, began to map out the strategy in which he saw the fulfillment of his desires for the conquered cities along the Atlantic seaboard. Moroni's plan to recover out of the hands of the Lamanites all Nephite territory in the…

control of the cities in his quarter of the land, Captain Moroni was having a more difficult time. Of particular concern was the city of Nephihah. Captain Moroni sent additional manpower to Nephihah, "knowing that it was easier to keep the city from falling into the hands of the Lamanites than to retake it from them" (Alma 59:9). 9-13 When Moroni saw that the City of Nephihah was lost he was exceeding sorrowful. Amid all his cares and preparations for the struggle before him, Moroni was forced to rely on those in governmental positions to lessen his task by fulfilling their allotted requirements. He had been assured, or at least led to think, that troops to the proper number would be sent to bolster the defenses of Nephihah. But when he learned that none had arrived there, and that because of inadequate manpower the…

9-13 When Moroni saw that the City of Nephihah was lost he was exceeding sorrowful. Amid all his cares and preparations for the struggle before him, Moroni was forced to rely on those in governmental positions to lessen his task by fulfilling their allotted requirements. Better to Prepare and Prevent than Repair and Repent Moroni asks Pahoran to strengthen the forces of Helaman-The Lamanites take the city of Nephihah-Moroni is angry with the government (Alma 59 headnote).
